diagrams.title: Gaussian Naïve Bayes Classification Pipeline
diagrams.description: Flowchart showing training and testing phases of Gaussian NB
elements: Training Data → Compute Priors P(C) by counting
Training Data → Compute Mean μ and Variance σ² for each feature per class using MLE
Test Instance → Compute Likelihood f(x|μ,σ²) for each feature
diagrams.purpose: Shows the complete workflow from training parameter estimation to final classification decision

diagrams.title: Gaussian Distribution for Two Classes
diagrams.description: Visualization of two Gaussian distributions for a single feature belonging to different classes
elements: Overlapping region shows uncertain classification area
Decision boundary at intersection point approximately x=12
diagrams.purpose: Illustrates how Gaussian NB uses overlapping distributions to compute likelihoods and shows how mean/variance affect classification uncertainty

diagrams.title: Naïve Bayes Independence Assumption Visualization
diagrams.description: Shows the concept of conditional independence compared to actual dependencies
elements: Left circle: Shows actual joint distribution P(X₁,X₂|C) with correlation
Right circle: Shows Naïve assumption as product of marginals P(X₁|C) × P(X₂|C)
Arrow showing conditional independence assumption: P(X₁,X₂|C) = P(X₁|C)P(X₂|C)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizes the key simplifying assumption and how it differs from reality, explaining why algorithm is called 'naïve'
